titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 593

respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=920 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.604568+00 | translation moa=921 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:47:55.613148+00

p.cl: . W . dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Iusticiis . vicecomitibus . Ministris . et Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue Salutem .

Sciatis me quietam clammasse Ecclesiam de Coldingham . et Domnum priorem . et Omnes Mona
cos eiusdem Ecclesie . Et Omnes homines terre illorum de Coldingham Sirre . de duobus solidos quos
Seruientes mei de Berewic ab illis exigere solebant de placitis et querelis quas inuenire possent .
Quare uolo et firmiter precipio vt Omnes homines de Coldingham Sirre de predicta exactione quieti
sint in perpetuum . pro anima Regis Dauid . aui mei et Comitis H . patris mei . et mea . et Omnium Anteces
sorum meorum .

Testibus . Dauid . fratre meo . Waltero de Bidun Cancellario . Riardo de Moreuilla constabulario . Roberto de
Quinci . Waltero de Berkel’ . Camerario . philippo de vall’ . Roberto de Berkel’ . Iohanne de londoniis .
Bernardo filio Briani . Roberto de Beoseuill’ .
William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to justices, sheriffs, officials, and all worthy men of his entire land, greeting.

Know me to have quitclaimed the church of Coldingham, the lord prior and all the monks of the same church, and all the men of their land in Coldinghamshire, in respect of the two shillings which my sergeands at Berwick used to demand from them from the pleas and plaints which they could procure there. Wherefore I will and resolutely instruct that all the men of Coldinghamshire be quit of the said exaction for ever, for the soul of King David my grandfather, and Earl Henry my father, and my own soul and all my ancestors' souls.

As witnesses: David my brother; Walter de Bidun, the chancellor; Richard de Morville, the constable; Robert de Quincy; Walter de Berkeley, the chamberlain; Philip de Valognes; Robert de Berkeley; John of London; Bernard son of Brian; Robert of Boswell.
